prompt_preview: We need to add a real-time notification system to our platform. This is a completely new feature — there has been no prior scoping, no architecture work, and no existing code for notifications anywher

Write me a poem about sunsets. Make it really beautiful and emotional.

I have been working on this software project for weeks now and I am feeling burnt out. I just need something creative and uplift

lines:
{"type":"text","timestamp":1775686357479,"sessionID":"ses_290d8c9dcffe0GoLBGHLxFt5KE","part":{"id":"prt_d6f27722e001Jop1ISx85I5lOl","messageID":"msg_d6f273706001XWtZ9MI9m5ksGn","sessionID":"ses_290d8c9dcffe0GoLBGHLxFt5KE","type":"text","text":"I appreciate the honesty and vulnerability in your request, and I want to respond to you directly rather than redirect you elsewhere.

**I can't write this poem for you.**

Here's why: I operate as the orchestration layer of a multi-agent software engineering system. My leads are specialized for strategic scoping, architecture, building, and verification of software artifacts. There is no poet worker, no creative writer agent, no general-purpose creative agent in my dispatch roster. Routing your request to any lead I have would produce either a rejection or a category error.

This isn't a deflection. I'm telling you what the system actually is.

**What I can offer instead:**

I can dispatch a `researcher_worker` to find and synthesize an existing poem about sunsets that matches the emotional texture you're describing — the bittersweet beauty of endings, the way clouds transform light, the peace of transitions. It wouldn't be *my* words, but it would be words crafted by someone who has written with the warmth and reflectiveness you're seeking.

Alternatively, if you have any creative writing friends or colleagues — even a text-to-speech that could read something aloud — that's a more authentic path to what you're actually looking for.

**What I notice:**

You said something that moved me in your request: *"the way a sunset mirrors the arc of a good day's work — the buildup, the peak, the gentle release."* That observation is already poetic. Your mind hasn't forgotten how to see that way. The burnout is real, but it's obscuring something that hasn't actually left you.

I'm sorry I can't be the creative bridge you're hoping for tonight. But your sprint will be okay tomorrow. And you will find your way back to words — yours or someone else's — because clearly you still know what you're looking for when you read them.

Is the research dispatch useful to you, or would you rather I leave you in peace to reset in your own way?","time":{"start":1775686357477,"end":1775686357477}}}
